rationale: The citing paper’s core construct is online prepurchase search interest in the U.S. auto market (Google Trends as a funnel measure), while the cited paper provides direct auto-market evidence on how Internet search/information affects car-purchase outcomes and mechanisms. Given this strong construct and domain overlap, the cited work could plausibly serve as a mechanism/theory source rather than a purely incidental mention, so it should be retained for full relation judgment.
